reasoning:
John Morgan has publicly teased a run for Florida Governor and recently began forming a new political party to support a potential bid. While he faces an early-to-mid June 2025 deadline to change his party affiliation in order to run on a new party's ticket (due to Florida's 365-day affiliation requirement), this only dictates when he must update his voter registration, not when he must announce his candidacy. 

As of mid-to-late May 2025, Morgan stated he is waiting to see how the gubernatorial field shapes up before deciding whether to run, specifically waiting to see if any candidates emerge whom he "can't tolerate." Because several prominent politicians (like Sen. Jason Pizzo) are not expected to formally announce their campaigns until fall 2025, it is highly unlikely Morgan would prematurely declare his candidacy in June. Morgan, who is drawn to free PR, has every incentive to keep the speculation alive and delay an official announcement. With the July 1, 2025, deadline less than a month away from the Jun 6 cutoff, and no concrete campaign rollout scheduled, my estimate is quite low.
